items.title: User prefers detailed reviews with code examples
items.content: When generating reports or reviews, include concrete code snippets and comparisons rather than abstract descriptions. User explicitly stated this preference.

items.title: Verify compilation after modifications
items.content: User wants /diff check + compilation verification (0 errors) + test run after any changes. Treat this as a mandatory post-modification step.

items.title: Shugu = Project_cc
items.content: Agent de coding IA inspiré de Claude Code, réécrit de zéro pour MiniMax M2.7

items.title: Architecture multi-agents
items.content: 14 outils intégrés (Bash, Read, Write, Edit, etc.), boucle principale avec turns/budget, transport HTTP MiniMax

items.title: Database choice
items.content: The team decided to use PostgreSQL over MongoDB for the main database because of complex relational queries needed.

items.title: Indentation preference
items.content: User prefers tabs over spaces with 2-width indentation in all TypeScript files.

items.title: API rate limit
items.content: The external payment service has a rate limit of 100 requests per minute. Above that, requests are throttled.

items.title: Deploy target
items.content: Production deployments go to Railway with auto-deploy from the main branch. Staging uses preview deploys.

items.title: Auth strategy
items.content: Authentication uses JWT tokens with 15-minute expiry and refresh tokens stored in httpOnly cookies.

items.title: Testing framework
items.content: The project uses Vitest for unit tests and Playwright for end-to-end testing.

items.title: CSS approach
items.content: Styling uses Tailwind CSS v4 with a custom design system. No CSS modules or styled-components.

items.title: Error monitoring
items.content: Sentry is configured for error monitoring in production with a 10% sample rate for performance traces.

items.title: Branch naming
items.content: Git branches follow the pattern type/description-YYYYMMDD where type is feat, fix, refactor, or chore.

items.title: API versioning
items.content: The REST API uses URL-based versioning with /api/v1/ prefix. GraphQL is at /graphql with schema versioning.

items.title: Cache strategy
items.content: Redis is used for caching with a 5-minute TTL for API responses and 1-hour TTL for user sessions.

items.title: Image processing
items.content: User uploads are processed through Sharp for resizing, stored in S3 with CloudFront CDN in front.

items.title: Logging format
items.content: Structured JSON logging via Pino. Log levels: error→warn→info→debug. Logs shipped to Datadog.

items.title: Email provider
items.content: Transactional emails sent via Resend. Marketing emails via Mailchimp. Templates in React Email.

items.title: State management
items.content: Frontend uses Zustand for global state and TanStack Query for server state. No Redux.

items.title: Search engine
items.content: Full-text search powered by Meilisearch with auto-sync from PostgreSQL every 30 seconds.

items.title: CI pipeline
items.content: GitHub Actions runs lint, typecheck, unit tests, and e2e tests on every PR. Deploys on merge to main.

items.title: Timezone handling
items.content: All dates stored in UTC. User timezone detected client-side via Intl API. Display uses date-fns-tz.

items.title: File uploads limit
items.content: Maximum file upload size is 50MB. Accepted formats: PNG, JPG, WebP, PDF, DOCX. Others rejected.

items.title: Feature flags
items.content: Feature flags managed through LaunchDarkly. New features behind flags for 2 weeks before full rollout.
